section.heading: Definitions

paragraph.heading: Burial ground.—
paragraph.content: The term “burial ground” means any natural or prepared physical location, whether originally below, on, or above the surface of the earth, into which human remains are deposited as a part of the death rite or ceremony of a culture.

paragraph.heading: Historic.—
paragraph.content: The term “historic”, with respect to a property, means a property that can reasonably be considered to date back at least 50 years.

paragraph.heading: Program.—
paragraph.content: The term “Program” means the United States African-American Burial Grounds Preservation Program established under section 308602(a).

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 117–328, div. DD, title VI, § 643(a), Dec. 29, 2022, 136 Stat. 5613.)
